Details: One study reported efficient cyanide removal from deionized water as well as a diluted industrial rinse water using ferrate(VI) [1360]. Ferrate(VI) oxidizes cyanide and generate cyanate as a byproduct. Cyanate will further react with ferrate(VI) and generate nitrite and carbon dioxide as final products. The reactions were reported to be accomplished in minutes. Almost 100 percent cyanide removal was reported by 27.6 mg/L ferrate(VI) with an initial cyanide concentration of 6 mg/L in deionized water.
